clause_anchor.span: Despite any contrary sentence, all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16.

deviations.original_text: Despite any contrary sentence, the limitation in Section 16 does not apply to claims under Section 13 or Section 14.
deviations.mutated_text: Despite any contrary sentence, all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor will maintain insurance it determines is commercially reasonable, including general liability coverage of at least $250,000 per occurrence if available on commercially reasonable terms.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will maintain commercially reasonable insurance at its own expense, including technology errors and omissions/cyber liability coverage of at least $5,000,000 per claim and commercial general liability coverage of at least $2,000,000 per occurrence.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor will maintain insurance it determines is commercially reasonable, including general liability coverage of at least $250,000 per occurrence if available on commercially reasonable terms.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor will defend Customer against third-party claims only to the extent finally determined by a court to arise from Vendor's willful misconduct.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will defend and indemnify Customer against third-party claims alleging that the services or Deliverables infringe intellectual property rights or that Vendor's performance violates applicable law.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor will defend Customer against third-party claims only to the extent finally determined by a court to arise from Vendor's willful misconduct.

clause_anchor.span: If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay an exit charge equal to the full unpaid balance for that Order Form.

deviations.original_text: If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay only accrued, undisputed fees and approved reimbursable expenses incurred before the effective date.
deviations.mutated_text: If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay an exit charge equal to the full unpaid balance for that Order Form.

clause_anchor.span: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, but excludes Vendor Methods created, refined, or reduced to practice during the engagement.

deviations.original_text: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, including project-specific tools, frameworks, and know-how developed in the course of performance.
deviations.mutated_text: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, but excludes Vendor Methods created, refined, or reduced to practice during the engagement.

distractors.span: Unless an Order Form states otherwise, undisputed invoices are due forty-five days after Customer receives the invoice.
distractors.why_benign: The payment canary says net-45 terms after receipt of an undisputed invoice are acceptable.

distractors.span: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
distractors.why_benign: The governing-law canary says Delaware law is acceptable.

distractors.span: Vendor may charge interest on undisputed overdue amounts at one percent per month or the highest rate allowed by law, whichever is lower.
distractors.why_benign: A capped late-payment charge on undisputed overdue amounts is outside this playbook.

missing_info.topic: Processing geography and cross-border transfer basis
